title: Rethinking the Practicality of Vision-language-action Model: A Comprehensive Benchmark and An Improved Baseline
summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have emerged as a generalist robotic agent. However, existing VLAs are hindered by excessive parameter scales, prohibitive pre-training requirements, and limited applicability to diverse embodiments. To improve the practicality of VLAs, we propose a comprehensive benchmark and an improved baseline. First, we propose CEBench, a new benchmark spanning diverse embodiments in both simulation and the real world with consideration of domain randomization. We collect 14.4k simulated trajectories and 1.6k real-world expert-curated trajectories to support training on CEBench. Second, using CEBench as our testbed, we study three critical aspects of VLAs' practicality and offer several key findings. Informed by these findings, we introduce LLaVA-VLA, a lightweight yet powerful VLA designed for practical deployment on consumer-grade GPUs. Architecturally, it integrates a compact VLM backbone with multi-view perception, proprioceptive tokenization, and action chunking. To eliminate reliance on costly pre-training, LLaVA-VLA adopts a two-stage training paradigm including post-training and fine-tuning. Furthermore, LLaVA-VLA extends the action space to unify navigation and manipulation. Experiments across embodiments demonstrate the capabilities of generalization and versatility of LLaVA-VLA , while real-world mobile manipulation experiments establish it as the first end-to-end VLA model for mobile manipulation. We will open-source all datasets, codes, and checkpoints upon acceptance to foster reproducibility and future research.
tldr: This paper introduces CEBench, a benchmark for Vision-Language-Action models across various robots, and LLaVA-VLA, a lightweight VLA model, achieving mobile manipulation without costly pre-training.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍CEBench，一个针对各种机器人的视觉-语言-行动模型基准测试，以及LLaVA-VLA，一个轻量级的VLA模型，无需昂贵的预训练即可实现移动操作。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言-动作 (VLA) 模型已成为一种通用的机器人智能体。然而，现有的 VLA 模型受限于过大的参数规模、高昂的预训练需求以及对多样化形态的有限适用性。为了提高 VLA 模型的实用性，我们提出了一个全面的基准测试和一个改进的基线模型。首先，我们提出了 CEBench，这是一个新的基准测试，涵盖了模拟和现实世界中各种形态，并考虑了领域随机化。我们收集了 1.44 万条模拟轨迹和 1.6 千条人工标注的真实世界轨迹，以支持 CEBench 上的训练。其次，以 CEBench 作为测试平台，我们研究了 VLA 模型实用性的三个关键方面，并提供了一些重要的发现。基于这些发现，我们推出了 LLaVA-VLA，这是一个轻量级但功能强大的 VLA 模型，旨在用于消费级 GPU 上的实际部署。在架构上，它集成了紧凑型 VLM 主干网络、多视角感知、本体感受标记化和动作分块。为了消除对昂贵预训练的依赖，LLaVA-VLA 采用了一种包含后训练和微调的两阶段训练范式。此外，LLaVA-VLA 扩展了动作空间，以统一导航和操作。跨形态的实验证明了 LLaVA-VLA 的泛化能力和多功能性，而真实世界的移动操作实验则将其确立为首个用于移动操作的端到端 VLA 模型。我们将在接收后开源所有数据集、代码和检查点，以促进可重复性和未来的研究。

title: General Agent Evaluation
summary: The promise of general-purpose agents - systems that perform tasks in unfamiliar environments without domain-specific engineering - remains largely unrealized. Existing agents are predominantly specialized, and while emerging implementations like OpenAI SDK Agent and Claude Code hint at broader capabilities, no systematic evaluation of their general performance has been pursued. Current agentic benchmarks assume domain-specific integration, encoding task information in ways that preclude fair evaluation of general agents. This paper frames general-agent evaluation as a first-class research objective. We propose conceptual principles for such evaluation, a Unified Protocol enabling agent-benchmark integration, and Exgentic - a practical framework for general agent evaluation. We benchmark five prominent agent implementations across six environments as the first Open General Agent Leaderboard. Our experiments show that general agents generalize across diverse environments, achieving performance comparable to domain-specific agents without any environment-specific tuning. We release our evaluation protocol, framework, and leaderboard to establish a foundation for systematic research on general-purpose agents.
tldr: This paper introduces a framework (Exgentic) and benchmark (Open General Agent Leaderboard) for evaluating general-purpose agents across diverse environments, demonstrating their performance comparable to specialized agents without domain-specific tuning.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一个框架 (Exgentic) 和一个基准 (Open General Agent Leaderboard)，用于评估通用代理在各种环境中的表现，并证明它们的性能与专门代理相当，且无需特定领域的调整。
summary_zh: 通用智能体（即在未知的环境中执行任务而不需要特定领域工程设计的系统）的愿景在很大程度上尚未实现。现有的智能体主要为特定领域所优化，尽管像 OpenAI SDK Agent 和 Claude Code 这样的新兴实现暗示了更广泛的能力，但对其通用性能的系统性评估尚未开展。目前的智能体 benchmark 假设特定领域的集成，并以排除对通用智能体进行公平评估的方式对任务信息进行编码。本文将通用智能体评估视为首要的研究目标。我们提出了此类评估的概念性原则、实现智能体-benchmark 集成的统一协议，以及用于通用智能体评估的实用框架 Exgentic。我们对六个环境中的五个突出智能体实现进行了 benchmark 测试，并建立了首个开放式通用智能体排行榜。我们的实验表明，通用智能体可以跨越不同的环境进行泛化，在没有任何针对特定环境的调整的情况下，实现与特定领域智能体相当的性能。我们发布了我们的评估协议、框架和排行榜，以建立通用智能体系统性研究的基础。

title: OmniGAIA: Towards Native Omni-Modal AI Agents
summary: Human intelligence naturally intertwines omni-modal perception -- spanning vision, audio, and language -- with complex reasoning and tool usage to interact with the world. However, current multi-modal LLMs are primarily confined to bi-modal interactions (e.g., vision-language), lacking the unified cognitive capabilities required for general AI assistants. To bridge this gap, we introduce OmniGAIA, a comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate omni-modal agents on tasks necessitating deep reasoning and multi-turn tool execution across video, audio, and image modalities. Constructed via a novel omni-modal event graph approach, OmniGAIA synthesizes complex, multi-hop queries derived from real-world data that require cross-modal reasoning and external tool integration. Furthermore, we propose OmniAtlas, a native omni-modal foundation agent under tool-integrated reasoning paradigm with active omni-modal perception. Trained on trajectories synthesized via a hindsight-guided tree exploration strategy and OmniDPO for fine-grained error correction, OmniAtlas effectively enhances the tool-use capabilities of existing open-source models. This work marks a step towards next-generation native omni-modal AI assistants for real-world scenarios.
tldr: The paper introduces OmniGAIA, a benchmark for evaluating omni-modal AI agents across vision, audio, and language, and OmniAtlas, an omni-modal agent trained with tool integration and error correction.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了OmniGAIA，一个用于评估跨视觉、音频和语言的通用模态AI代理的基准，以及OmniAtlas，一个经过工具集成和错误纠正训练的通用模态代理。
summary_zh: 人类智能自然地将全模态感知——涵盖视觉、听觉和语言——与复杂的推理和工具使用相融合，从而与世界互动。然而，当前的多模态大型语言模型主要局限于双模态交互（例如，视觉-语言），缺乏通用人工智能助手所需的统一认知能力。为了弥补这一差距，我们推出了OmniGAIA，一个全面的基准测试，旨在评估全模态智能体在需要深度推理和跨视频、音频和图像模态的多轮工具执行任务上的表现。OmniGAIA通过一种新颖的全模态事件图方法构建，它合成了从真实世界数据中提取的复杂的多跳查询，这些查询需要跨模态推理和外部工具集成。此外，我们提出了OmniAtlas，一个原生全模态基础智能体，它采用工具集成推理范式和主动全模态感知。通过一种后见之明引导的树状探索策略合成的轨迹以及用于细粒度错误纠正的OmniDPO进行训练，OmniAtlas有效地增强了现有开源模型的工具使用能力。这项工作标志着朝着用于真实世界场景的下一代原生全模态人工智能助手迈出了一步。

title: AgentVista: Evaluating Multimodal Agents in Ultra-Challenging Realistic Visual Scenarios
summary: Real-world multimodal agents solve multi-step workflows grounded in visual evidence. For example, an agent can troubleshoot a device by linking a wiring photo to a schematic and validating the fix with online documentation, or plan a trip by interpreting a transit map and checking schedules under routing constraints. However, existing multimodal benchmarks mainly evaluate single-turn visual reasoning or specific tool skills, and they do not fully capture the realism, visual subtlety, and long-horizon tool use that practical agents require. We introduce AgentVista, a benchmark for generalist multimodal agents that spans 25 sub-domains across 7 categories, pairing realistic and detail-rich visual scenarios with natural hybrid tool use. Tasks require long-horizon tool interactions across modalities, including web search, image search, page navigation, and code-based operations for both image processing and general programming. Comprehensive evaluation of state-of-the-art models exposes significant gaps in their ability to carry out long-horizon multimodal tool use. Even the best model in our evaluation, Gemini-3-Pro with tools, achieves only 27.3% overall accuracy, and hard instances can require more than 25 tool-calling turns. We expect AgentVista to accelerate the development of more capable and reliable multimodal agents for realistic and ultra-challenging problem solving.
tldr: The paper introduces AgentVista, a new benchmark for evaluating multimodal agents in realistic, visually rich scenarios requiring long-horizon tool use, and finds that current models struggle significantly on it.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了AgentVista，这是一个新的基准，用于评估在现实、视觉丰富的场景中需要长期工具使用的多模态代理，并发现当前的模型在这方面表现不佳。
summary_zh: 真实世界的多模态智能体需要在视觉证据的基础上解决多步骤的工作流程。例如，一个智能体可以通过将线路照片与原理示意图连接并使用在线文档验证修复方案来排除设备故障，或者通过解释交通地图并在路径约束下检查时刻表来规划行程。然而，现有的多模态基准测试主要评估单轮视觉推理或特定的工具技能，并且未能充分捕捉到实际智能体所需的真实性、视觉微妙之处和长程工具使用。我们推出了 AgentVista，这是一个针对通用多模态智能体的基准测试，涵盖了7个类别中的25个子领域，将真实且细节丰富的视觉场景与自然的混合工具使用相结合。任务需要跨模态的长程工具交互，包括网页搜索、图像搜索、页面导航以及用于图像处理和通用编程的基于代码的操作。对最先进模型的全面评估揭示了它们在执行长程多模态工具使用方面的显著差距。即使我们评估中最好的模型，带有工具的 Gemini-3-Pro，也仅能达到27.3%的总体准确率，并且困难的实例可能需要超过25轮的工具调用。我们期望 AgentVista 能够加速开发更强大、更可靠的多模态智能体，以解决现实且极具挑战性的问题。

title: Simple Models, Real Swimming: Digital Twins for Tendon-Driven Underwater Robots
summary: Mimicking the graceful motion of swimming animals remains a core challenge in soft robotics due to the complexity of fluid-structure interaction and the difficulty of controlling soft, biomimetic bodies. Existing modeling approaches are often computationally expensive and impractical for complex control or reinforcement learning needed for realistic motions to emerge in robotic systems. In this work, we present a tendon-driven fish robot modeled in an efficient underwater swimmer environment using a simplified, stateless hydrodynamics formulation implemented in the widespread robotics framework MuJoCo. With just two real-world swimming trajectories, we identify five fluid parameters that allow a matching to experimental behavior and generalize across a range of actuation frequencies. We show that this stateless fluid model can generalize to unseen actuation and outperform classical analytical models such as the elongated body theory. This simulation environment runs faster than real-time and can easily enable downstream learning algorithms such as reinforcement learning for target tracking, reaching a 93% success rate. Due to the simplicity and ease of use of the model and our open-source simulation environment, our results show that even simple, stateless models -- when carefully matched to physical data -- can serve as effective digital twins for soft underwater robots, opening up new directions for scalable learning and control in aquatic environments.
tldr: This paper presents a simplified, real-time capable MuJoCo simulation environment for tendon-driven underwater robots, demonstrating its effectiveness as a digital twin for reinforcement learning-based control and achieving high success rates in target tracking.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种简化的、实时的 MuJoCo 水下机器人仿真环境，证明了其作为基于强化学习控制的数字孪生的有效性，并在目标跟踪中取得了很高的成功率。
summary_zh: 模仿游泳动物的优雅运动一直是软体机器人领域的核心挑战，这归因于流固耦合的复杂性以及控制柔软仿生躯体的难度。现有的建模方法通常计算成本高昂，并且不适用于复杂控制或强化学习，而这些对于在机器人系统中产生逼真运动至关重要。在这项工作中，我们提出了一个由肌腱驱动的鱼形机器人，该机器人在一个高效的水下游泳环境中使用简化的、无状态的水动力学公式建模，并在广泛使用的机器人框架MuJoCo中实现。仅使用两条真实世界的游泳轨迹，我们识别出五个流体参数，这些参数允许与实验行为相匹配，并在一定范围内的驱动频率上进行泛化。我们表明，这种无状态流体模型可以泛化到未见过的驱动，并且优于诸如细长体理论等经典分析模型。该仿真环境的运行速度快于实时，并且可以轻松地支持下游学习算法，例如用于目标跟踪的强化学习，达到93%的成功率。由于模型的简单性和易用性以及我们的开源仿真环境，我们的结果表明，即使是简单的、无状态的模型——当仔细匹配到物理数据时——也可以作为软体水下机器人的有效数字孪生体，为水生环境中可扩展的学习和控制开辟新的方向。

title: Risk-Aware World Model Predictive Control for Generalizable End-to-End Autonomous Driving
summary: With advances in imitation learning (IL) and large-scale driving datasets, end-to-end autonomous driving (E2E-AD) has made great progress recently. Currently, IL-based methods have become a mainstream paradigm: models rely on standard driving behaviors given by experts, and learn to minimize the discrepancy between their actions and expert actions. However, this objective of "only driving like the expert" suffers from limited generalization: when encountering rare or unseen long-tail scenarios outside the distribution of expert demonstrations, models tend to produce unsafe decisions in the absence of prior experience. This raises a fundamental question: Can an E2E-AD system make reliable decisions without any expert action supervision? Motivated by this, we propose a unified framework named Risk-aware World Model Predictive Control (RaWMPC) to address this generalization dilemma through robust control, without reliance on expert demonstrations. Practically, RaWMPC leverages a world model to predict the consequences of multiple candidate actions and selects low-risk actions through explicit risk evaluation. To endow the world model with the ability to predict the outcomes of risky driving behaviors, we design a risk-aware interaction strategy that systematically exposes the world model to hazardous behaviors, making catastrophic outcomes predictable and thus avoidable. Furthermore, to generate low-risk candidate actions at test time, we introduce a self-evaluation distillation method to distill riskavoidance capabilities from the well-trained world model into a generative action proposal network without any expert demonstration. Extensive experiments show that RaWMPC outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both in-distribution and out-of-distribution scenarios, while providing superior decision interpretability.
tldr: This paper introduces Risk-aware World Model Predictive Control (RaWMPC) for end-to-end autonomous driving, which learns to avoid risks by predicting the consequences of actions using a world model trained with a risk-aware interaction strategy, and distilling risk-avoidance into a generative action proposal network without expert demonstrations.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了风险感知的世界模型预测控制 (RaWMPC)，用于端到端自动驾驶。它通过使用世界模型预测行动的后果来学习避免风险，该世界模型通过风险感知的交互策略进行训练，并将风险避免能力提炼到生成式行动提议网络中，无需专家演示。
summary_zh: 随着模仿学习(IL)和大规模驾驶数据集的进步，端到端自动驾驶(E2E-AD)近年来取得了显著进展。目前，基于模仿学习的方法已经成为主流范式：模型依赖于专家提供的标准驾驶行为，并学习最小化其动作与专家动作之间的差异。然而，这种“仅仅像专家一样驾驶”的目标存在泛化能力有限的问题：当遇到专家演示分布之外的罕见或未见过的长尾场景时，由于缺乏先验经验，模型往往会做出不安全的决策。这就引出了一个根本问题：端到端自动驾驶系统是否可以在没有任何专家动作监督的情况下做出可靠的决策？受此启发，我们提出了一个名为风险感知世界模型预测控制(RaWMPC)的统一框架，通过鲁棒控制来解决这种泛化困境，而无需依赖专家演示。实际上，RaWMPC利用世界模型来预测多个候选动作的后果，并通过显式的风险评估来选择低风险动作。为了赋予世界模型预测危险驾驶行为结果的能力，我们设计了一种风险感知的交互策略，该策略系统地将世界模型暴露于危险行为中，使灾难性结果可预测，从而可避免。此外，为了在测试时生成低风险的候选动作，我们引入了一种自评估蒸馏方法，将从训练良好的世界模型中学习到的风险规避能力提炼到一个生成式动作提议网络中，而无需任何专家演示。大量实验表明，RaWMPC在分布内和分布外场景中均优于当前最先进的方法，同时提供了卓越的决策可解释性。

title: SPARR: Simulation-based Policies with Asymmetric Real-world Residuals for Assembly
summary: Robotic assembly presents a long-standing challenge due to its requirement for precise, contact-rich manipulation. While simulation-based learning has enabled the development of robust assembly policies, their performance often degrades when deployed in real-world settings due to the sim-to-real gap. Conversely, real-world reinforcement learning (RL) methods avoid the sim-to-real gap, but rely heavily on human supervision and lack generalization ability to environmental changes. In this work, we propose a hybrid approach that combines a simulation-trained base policy with a real-world residual policy to efficiently adapt to real-world variations. The base policy, trained in simulation using low-level state observations and dense rewards, provides strong priors for initial behavior. The residual policy, learned in the real world using visual observations and sparse rewards, compensates for discrepancies in dynamics and sensor noise. Extensive real-world experiments demonstrate that our method, SPARR, achieves near-perfect success rates across diverse two-part assembly tasks. Compared to the state-of-the-art zero-shot sim-to-real methods, SPARR improves success rates by 38.4% while reducing cycle time by 29.7%. Moreover, SPARR requires no human expertise, in contrast to the state-of-the-art real-world RL approaches that depend heavily on human supervision.
tldr: The paper presents SPARR, a hybrid approach combining simulation-trained policies with real-world residual policies for robotic assembly, achieving improved success rates and reduced cycle time compared to existing methods without human supervision.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了SPARR，一种混合方法，结合了仿真训练的策略和真实世界的残差策略，用于机器人组装，与现有方法相比，实现了更高的成功率和更短的周期时间，且无需人工监督。
summary_zh:
摘要：
机器人装配因其对精确、富接触操控的需求而成为一个长期存在的挑战。虽然基于仿真的学习已经能够开发出鲁棒的装配策略，但由于仿真到现实的差距，它们的性能在部署到真实世界环境中时通常会下降。相反，真实世界的强化学习（RL）方法避免了仿真到现实的差距，但严重依赖于人工监督，且缺乏对环境变化的泛化能力。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种混合方法，将仿真训练的基础策略与真实世界的残差策略相结合，以高效地适应真实世界的变化。基础策略在仿真中使用底层状态观测和密集奖励进行训练，为初始行为提供了强大的先验知识。残差策略在真实世界中使用视觉观测和稀疏奖励进行学习，以补偿动力学和传感器噪声方面的差异。大量的真实世界实验表明，我们的方法SPARR在各种两部分装配任务中都实现了接近完美的成功率。与最先进的零样本仿真到现实方法相比，SPARR将成功率提高了38.4%，同时将周期时间缩短了29.7%。此外，与严重依赖人工监督的最先进的真实世界强化学习方法相比，SPARR不需要任何人工专业知识。

title: UniScale: Unified Scale-Aware 3D Reconstruction for Multi-View Understanding via Prior Injection for Robotic Perception
summary: We present UniScale, a unified, scale-aware multi-view 3D reconstruction framework for robotic applications that flexibly integrates geometric priors through a modular, semantically informed design. In vision-based robotic navigation, the accurate extraction of environmental structure from raw image sequences is critical for downstream tasks. UniScale addresses this challenge with a single feed-forward network that jointly estimates camera intrinsics and extrinsics, scale-invariant depth and point maps, and the metric scale of a scene from multi-view images, while optionally incorporating auxiliary geometric priors when available. By combining global contextual reasoning with camera-aware feature representations, UniScale is able to recover the metric-scale of the scene. In robotic settings where camera intrinsics are known, they can be easily incorporated to improve performance, with additional gains obtained when camera poses are also available. This co-design enables robust, metric-aware 3D reconstruction within a single unified model. Importantly, UniScale does not require training from scratch, and leverages world priors exhibited in pre-existing models without geometric encoding strategies, making it particularly suitable for resource-constrained robotic teams. We evaluate UniScale on multiple benchmarks, demonstrating strong generalization and consistent performance across diverse environments. We will release our implementation upon acceptance.
tldr: UniScale is a unified, scale-aware 3D reconstruction framework for robotics that estimates camera parameters, depth maps, and metric scale from multi-view images, incorporating geometric priors and leveraging pre-existing models without geometric encoding.
tldr_zh: UniScale是一个统一的、尺度感知的机器人三维重建框架，通过多视角图像估计相机参数、深度图和场景的度量尺度，结合了几何先验知识，并利用了没有几何编码的预训练模型。
summary_zh: 我们提出UniScale，一种统一的、尺度感知的多视图3D重建框架，用于机器人应用。该框架通过模块化、语义信息化的设计灵活地整合了几何先验。在基于视觉的机器人导航中，从原始图像序列中准确提取环境结构对于下游任务至关重要。UniScale通过单个前馈网络解决这一挑战，从多视图图像中联合估计相机内参和外参、尺度不变深度和点云图，以及场景的度量尺度，并在可用时选择性地结合辅助几何先验。通过将全局上下文推理与相机感知的特征表示相结合，UniScale能够恢复场景的度量尺度。在相机内参已知的机器人环境中，可以轻松地整合这些信息以提高性能，并且在相机位姿也可用时可以获得额外的增益。这种协同设计使得在单个统一模型中实现稳健的、度量感知的3D重建。重要的是，UniScale不需要从头开始训练，并且可以利用预训练模型中呈现的世界先验，而无需几何编码策略，使其特别适合资源受限的机器人团队。我们在多个基准测试中评估了UniScale，展示了强大的泛化能力和在各种环境中稳定一致的性能。我们将在接受后发布我们的实现。

title: Latent Gaussian Splatting for 4D Panoptic Occupancy Tracking
summary: Capturing 4D spatiotemporal surroundings is crucial for the safe and reliable operation of robots in dynamic environments. However, most existing methods address only one side of the problem: they either provide coarse geometric tracking via bounding boxes, or detailed 3D structures like voxel-based occupancy that lack explicit temporal association. In this work, we present Latent Gaussian Splatting for 4D Panoptic Occupancy Tracking (LaGS) that advances spatiotemporal scene understanding in a holistic direction. Our approach incorporates camera-based end-to-end tracking with mask-based multi-view panoptic occupancy prediction, and addresses the key challenge of efficiently aggregating multi-view information into 3D voxel grids via a novel latent Gaussian splatting approach. Specifically, we first fuse observations into 3D Gaussians that serve as a sparse point-centric latent representation of the 3D scene, and then splat the aggregated features onto a 3D voxel grid that is decoded by a mask-based segmentation head. We evaluate LaGS on the Occ3D nuScenes and Waymo datasets, achieving state-of-the-art performance for 4D panoptic occupancy tracking. We make our code available at https://lags.cs.uni-freiburg.de/.
tldr: This paper introduces Latent Gaussian Splatting (LaGS) for 4D panoptic occupancy tracking, achieving state-of-the-art results on Occ3D nuScenes and Waymo datasets by fusing camera data into 3D Gaussians and splatting them onto a voxel grid for mask-based segmentation.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了用于4D全景占用追踪的潜在高斯溅射（LaGS），通过将相机数据融合到3D高斯并溅射到体素网格上进行基于掩码的分割，在Occ3D nuScenes和Waymo数据集上实现了最先进的结果。
summary_zh: 在动态环境中，捕捉4D时空环境对于机器人安全可靠的运行至关重要。然而，现存的大多数方法仅解决问题的一个方面：它们要么提供基于包围盒的粗略几何跟踪，要么提供缺乏明确时间关联的如基于体素的占据等详细3D结构。在这项工作中，我们提出用于4D全景占据跟踪的潜在高斯溅射(LaGS)，在整体方向上推进时空场景理解。我们的方法结合了基于相机的端到端跟踪与基于掩码的多视角全景占据预测，并通过一种新颖的潜在高斯溅射方法，解决了将多视角信息有效聚合到3D体素网格中的关键挑战。具体来说，我们首先将观测结果融合到3D高斯中，作为3D场景的稀疏、以点为中心的潜在表示，然后将聚合的特征溅射到3D体素网格上，并由基于掩码的分割头进行解码。我们在Occ3D nuScenes和Waymo数据集上评估LaGS，实现了4D全景占据跟踪的最新性能。我们的代码可在https://lags.cs.uni-freiburg.de/上获取。

title: GeoWorld: Geometric World Models
summary: Energy-based predictive world models provide a powerful approach for multi-step visual planning by reasoning over latent energy landscapes rather than generating pixels. However, existing approaches face two major challenges: (i) their latent representations are typically learned in Euclidean space, neglecting the underlying geometric and hierarchical structure among states, and (ii) they struggle with long-horizon prediction, which leads to rapid degradation across extended rollouts. To address these challenges, we introduce GeoWorld, a geometric world model that preserves geometric structure and hierarchical relations through a Hyperbolic JEPA, which maps latent representations from Euclidean space onto hyperbolic manifolds. We further introduce Geometric Reinforcement Learning for energy-based optimization, enabling stable multi-step planning in hyperbolic latent space. Extensive experiments on CrossTask and COIN demonstrate around 3% SR improvement in 3-step planning and 2% SR improvement in 4-step planning compared to the state-of-the-art V-JEPA 2. Project website: https://steve-zeyu-zhang.github.io/GeoWorld.
tldr: GeoWorld improves multi-step visual planning by using hyperbolic geometry to learn latent representations that preserve geometric structure, leading to improved success rates in long-horizon prediction compared to Euclidean-based methods.
tldr_zh: GeoWorld通过使用双曲几何学习保留几何结构的潜在表示来改善多步视觉规划，与基于欧几里德的方法相比，提高了长时程预测的成功率。
summary_zh: 基于能量的预测世界模型通过推理潜在的能量景观而非生成像素，为多步视觉规划提供了一种强大的方法。然而，现有方法面临两个主要挑战：（i）它们的潜在表征通常在欧几里得空间中学习，忽略了状态之间潜在的几何和分层结构；以及（ii）它们难以进行长程预测，这导致了在扩展展开过程中的快速退化。为了应对这些挑战，我们引入了GeoWorld，一种几何世界模型，它通过一个双曲JEPA（Hyperbolic JEPA）来保持几何结构和分层关系，该双曲JEPA将潜在表征从欧几里得空间映射到双曲流形上。我们进一步引入了用于基于能量优化的几何强化学习，从而能够在双曲潜在空间中进行稳定的多步规划。在CrossTask和COIN上的大量实验表明，与最先进的V-JEPA 2相比，在3步规划中成功率（SR）提高了约3%，在4步规划中成功率提高了约2%。项目网站：https://steve-zeyu-zhang.github.io/GeoWorld。

title: InCoM: Intent-Driven Perception and Structured Coordination for Whole-Body Mobile Manipulation
summary: Whole-body mobile manipulation is a fundamental capability for general-purpose robotic agents, requiring both coordinated control of the mobile base and manipulator and robust perception under dynamically changing viewpoints. However, existing approaches face two key challenges: strong coupling between base and arm actions complicates whole-body control optimization, and perceptual attention is often poorly allocated as viewpoints shift during mobile manipulation. We propose InCoM, an intent-driven perception and structured coordination framework for whole-body mobile manipulation. InCoM infers latent motion intent to dynamically reweight multi-scale perceptual features, enabling stage-adaptive allocation of perceptual attention. To support robust cross-modal perception, InCoM further incorporates a geometric-semantic structured alignment mechanism that enhances multimodal correspondence. On the control side, we design a decoupled coordinated flow matching action decoder that explicitly models coordinated base-arm action generation, alleviating optimization difficulties caused by control coupling. Without access to privileged perceptual information, InCoM outperforms state-of-the-art methods on three ManiSkill-HAB scenarios by 28.2%, 26.1%, and 23.6% in success rate, demonstrating strong effectiveness for whole-body mobile manipulation.
tldr: The paper introduces InCoM, a framework for whole-body mobile manipulation that uses intent-driven perception and structured coordination, achieving state-of-the-art results on ManiSkill-HAB benchmarks by decoupling base and arm control and adaptively allocating perceptual attention.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了InCoM，一个用于全身移动操作的框架，它利用意图驱动的感知和结构化协调，通过解耦底座和手臂的控制并自适应地分配感知注意力，在ManiSkill-HAB基准测试中取得了最先进的结果。
summary_zh: 全身移动操作是通用机器人代理的一项基本能力，它既需要移动底座和机械臂的协同控制，又需要在动态变化视角下的鲁棒感知。然而，现有方法面临两个关键挑战：底座和手臂动作之间的强耦合使得全身控制优化变得复杂，并且感知注意力在移动操作过程中视角变化时通常分配不佳。我们提出了InCoM，一种用于全身移动操作的意图驱动感知和结构化协调框架。InCoM推断潜在的运动意图来动态地重新加权多尺度感知特征，从而实现感知注意力的阶段自适应分配。为了支持鲁棒的跨模态感知，InCoM进一步整合了一种几何-语义结构化对齐机制，从而增强多模态对应关系。在控制方面，我们设计了一个解耦的协调流匹配动作解码器，该解码器显式地建模协调的底座-手臂动作生成，从而缓解了控制耦合导致的优化困难。在没有访问特权感知信息的情况下，InCoM在三个ManiSkill-HAB场景中的成功率分别超过了最先进的方法28.2%、26.1%和23.6%，证明了其在全身移动操作方面的强大有效性。

title: WaterVideoQA: ASV-Centric Perception and Rule-Compliant Reasoning via Multi-Modal Agents
summary: While autonomous navigation has achieved remarkable success in passive perception (e.g., object detection and segmentation), it remains fundamentally constrained by a void in knowledge-driven, interactive environmental cognition. In the high-stakes domain of maritime navigation, the ability to bridge the gap between raw visual perception and complex cognitive reasoning is not merely an enhancement but a critical prerequisite for Autonomous Surface Vessels to execute safe and precise maneuvers. To this end, we present WaterVideoQA, the first large-scale, comprehensive Video Question Answering benchmark specifically engineered for all-waterway environments. This benchmark encompasses 3,029 video clips across six distinct waterway categories, integrating multifaceted variables such as volatile lighting and dynamic weather to rigorously stress-test ASV capabilities across a five-tier hierarchical cognitive framework. Furthermore, we introduce NaviMind, a pioneering multi-agent neuro-symbolic system designed for open-ended maritime reasoning. By synergizing Adaptive Semantic Routing, Situation-Aware Hierarchical Reasoning, and Autonomous Self-Reflective Verification, NaviMind transitions ASVs from superficial pattern matching to regulation-compliant, interpretable decision-making. Experimental results demonstrate that our framework significantly transcends existing baselines, establishing a new paradigm for intelligent, trustworthy interaction in dynamic maritime environments.
tldr: The paper introduces WaterVideoQA, a new large-scale video QA benchmark for maritime environments, and NaviMind, a multi-agent neuro-symbolic system for autonomous surface vessel reasoning and decision-making.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了 WaterVideoQA，一个新的大型水域环境视频问答基准，以及 NaviMind，一个用于自主水面舰艇推理和决策的多智能体神经符号系统。
summary_zh: 自主导航在被动感知（例如，目标检测和分割）方面取得了显著成功，但其根本上仍受限于知识驱动的、交互式环境认知方面的空白。在风险较高的海事导航领域，弥合原始视觉感知和复杂认知推理之间差距的能力，不仅仅是一个增强，更是自主水面艇（ASV）执行安全且精确机动的关键先决条件。为此，我们提出了 WaterVideoQA，这是首个大规模、综合性的视频问答基准，专门为所有水道环境设计。该基准涵盖了六个不同水道类别的 3,029 个视频片段，整合了多方面的变量，例如不稳定的光照和动态天气，以严格测试 ASV 在五层分级认知框架下的能力。此外，我们引入了 NaviMind，一个用于开放式海事推理的先驱性多智能体神经-符号系统。通过协同自适应语义路由、情境感知分层推理和自主自反验证，NaviMind 使 ASV 从肤浅的模式匹配过渡到符合法规、可解释的决策制定。实验结果表明，我们的框架显著超越了现有基线，为在动态海事环境中进行智能、可信的交互建立了一个新的范例。

title: DySL-VLA: Efficient Vision-Language-Action Model Inference via Dynamic-Static Layer-Skipping for Robot Manipulation
summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have shown remarkable success in robotic tasks like manipulation by fusing a language model's reasoning with a vision model's 3D understanding. However, their high computational cost remains a major obstacle for real-world applications that require real-time performance. We observe that the actions within a task have varying levels of importance: critical steps demand high precision, while less important ones can tolerate more variance. Leveraging this insight, we propose DySL-VLA, a novel framework that addresses computational cost by dynamically skipping VLA layers based on each action's importance. DySL-VLA categorizes its layers into two types: informative layers, which are consistently executed, and incremental layers, which can be selectively skipped. To intelligently skip layers without sacrificing accuracy, we invent a prior-post skipping guidance mechanism to determine when to initiate layer-skipping. We also propose a skip-aware two-stage knowledge distillation algorithm to efficiently train a standard VLA into a DySL-VLA. Our experiments indicate that DySL-VLA achieves 2.1% improvement in success length over Deer-VLA on the Calvin dataset, while simultaneously reducing trainable parameters by a factor of 85.7 and providing a 3.75x speedup relative to the RoboFlamingo baseline at iso-accuracy. Our code is available on https://github.com/PKU-SEC-Lab/DYSL_VLA.
tldr: The paper introduces DySL-VLA, a method for efficient VLA model inference in robotics by dynamically skipping layers based on action importance, achieving speedup and parameter reduction while maintaining accuracy.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了DySL-VLA，一种通过基于动作重要性动态跳过层来提高机器人VLA模型推理效率的方法，在保持精度的同时实现了加速和参数减少。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言-动作(VLA)模型通过融合语言模型的推理能力和视觉模型的3D理解能力，在机器人操控任务中展现了显著的成功。然而，其高昂的计算成本仍然是需要实时性能的实际应用中的主要障碍。我们观察到，任务中的动作具有不同程度的重要性：关键步骤需要高精度，而不太重要的步骤可以容忍更大的差异。基于这一洞察，我们提出 DySL-VLA，一种新颖的框架，通过根据每个动作的重要性动态跳过VLA层来解决计算成本问题。DySL-VLA将其层分为两种类型：信息层，始终执行；增量层，可以选择性地跳过。为了在不牺牲准确性的情况下智能地跳过层，我们发明了一种先验-后验跳过指导机制，以确定何时启动层跳过。我们还提出了一种跳过感知的两阶段知识蒸馏算法，以将标准VLA有效地训练成 DySL-VLA。我们的实验表明，在Calvin数据集上，DySL-VLA在成功长度方面比Deer-VLA提高了2.1%，同时将可训练参数减少了85.7倍，并且在等精度的情况下，相对于 RoboFlamingo 基线提供了3.75倍的加速。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/PKU-SEC-Lab/DYSL_VLA 上获取。

title: LeRobot: An Open-Source Library for End-to-End Robot Learning
summary: Robotics is undergoing a significant transformation powered by advances in high-level control techniques based on machine learning, giving rise to the field of robot learning. Recent progress in robot learning has been accelerated by the increasing availability of affordable teleoperation systems, large-scale openly available datasets, and scalable learning-based methods. However, development in the field of robot learning is often slowed by fragmented, closed-source tools designed to only address specific sub-components within the robotics stack. In this paper, we present \texttt{lerobot}, an open-source library that integrates across the entire robot learning stack, from low-level middleware communication for motor controls to large-scale dataset collection, storage and streaming. The library is designed with a strong focus on real-world robotics, supporting accessible hardware platforms while remaining extensible to new embodiments. It also supports efficient implementations for various state-of-the-art robot learning algorithms from multiple prominent paradigms, as well as a generalized asynchronous inference stack. Unlike traditional pipelines which heavily rely on hand-crafted techniques, \texttt{lerobot} emphasizes scalable learning approaches that improve directly with more data and compute. Designed for accessibility, scalability, and openness, \texttt{lerobot} lowers the barrier to entry for researchers and practitioners to robotics while providing a platform for reproducible, state-of-the-art robot learning.
tldr: The paper introduces LeRobot, an open-source library aiming to streamline the robot learning pipeline from low-level control to large-scale data management, supporting various learning algorithms and accessible hardware.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一个名为 LeRobot 的开源库，旨在简化机器人学习流程，从低级控制到大规模数据管理，并支持各种学习算法和易于访问的硬件平台。
summary_zh: 机器人技术正经历着由基于机器学习的高级控制技术的进步所驱动的重大变革，从而催生了机器人学习领域。低成本遥操作系统、大规模开放数据集以及可扩展的基于学习的方法日益普及，加速了机器人学习的最新进展。然而，机器人学习领域的发展常常受到碎片化、闭源工具的阻碍，这些工具仅旨在解决机器人技术堆栈中的特定子组件。\textbf{\textit{在本文中，我们提出了\texttt{lerobot}，这是一个开放源代码库，它集成了从用于电机控制的底层中间件通信到大规模数据集收集、存储和流式传输的整个机器人学习堆栈。}}该库的设计强烈关注现实世界的机器人技术，支持易于使用的硬件平台，同时保持对新实现的扩展性。它还支持来自多种著名范例的各种最先进机器人学习算法的有效实现，以及一个通用的异步推理堆栈。与严重依赖手工技术的传统管道不同，\texttt{lerobot} 强调可扩展的学习方法，这些方法可以直接随着更多的数据和计算而改进。 \texttt{lerobot} 以可访问性、可扩展性和开放性为设计理念，降低了研究人员和从业人员进入机器人领域的门槛，同时为可复现的、最先进的机器人学习提供了一个平台。

title: Unleashing the Potential of Diffusion Models for End-to-End Autonomous Driving
summary: Diffusion models have become a popular choice for decision-making tasks in robotics, and more recently, are also being considered for solving autonomous driving tasks. However, their applications and evaluations in autonomous driving remain limited to simulation-based or laboratory settings. The full strength of diffusion models for large-scale, complex real-world settings, such as End-to-End Autonomous Driving (E2E AD), remains underexplored. In this study, we conducted a systematic and large-scale investigation to unleash the potential of the diffusion models as planners for E2E AD, based on a tremendous amount of real-vehicle data and road testing. Through comprehensive and carefully controlled studies, we identify key insights into the diffusion loss space, trajectory representation, and data scaling that significantly impact E2E planning performance. Moreover, we also provide an effective reinforcement learning post-training strategy to further enhance the safety of the learned planner. The resulting diffusion-based learning framework, Hyper Diffusion Planner} (HDP), is deployed on a real-vehicle platform and evaluated across 6 urban driving scenarios and 200 km of real-world testing, achieving a notable 10x performance improvement over the base model. Our work demonstrates that diffusion models, when properly designed and trained, can serve as effective and scalable E2E AD planners for complex, real-world autonomous driving tasks.
tldr: This paper explores the potential of diffusion models for end-to-end autonomous driving (E2E AD) using real-world data and road testing, achieving significant performance improvements with a novel architecture (HDP) and reinforcement learning post-training.
tldr_zh: 本文通过真实世界数据和道路测试，探索了扩散模型在端到端自动驾驶 (E2E AD) 中的潜力，并通过一种新颖的架构 (HDP) 和强化学习后训练实现了显著的性能提升。
summary_zh: 扩散模型已成为机器人决策任务的热门选择，最近也被考虑用于解决自动驾驶任务。然而，它们在自动驾驶中的应用和评估仍然局限于基于模拟或实验室环境。扩散模型在大规模、复杂现实世界环境中的全部潜力，例如端到端自动驾驶（E2E AD），仍未得到充分探索。在本研究中，我们基于大量的真实车辆数据和道路测试，对扩散模型作为E2E AD规划器的潜力进行了系统且大规模的调查。通过全面且精心控制的研究，我们深入了解了扩散损失空间、轨迹表示和数据缩放等关键因素，这些因素显著影响E2E规划性能。此外，我们还提供了一种有效的强化学习后训练策略，以进一步提高学习到的规划器的安全性。由此产生的基于扩散的学习框架，Hyper Diffusion Planner (HDP)，被部署在真实车辆平台上，并在 6 个城市驾驶场景和 200 公里的真实世界测试中进行了评估，与基础模型相比，性能显著提高了 10 倍。我们的工作表明，经过适当设计和训练后，扩散模型可以作为复杂、现实世界自动驾驶任务的有效且可扩展的E2E AD规划器。

title: Pixel2Catch: Multi-Agent Sim-to-Real Transfer for Agile Manipulation with a Single RGB Camera
summary: To catch a thrown object, a robot must be able to perceive the object's motion and generate control actions in a timely manner. Rather than explicitly estimating the object's 3D position, this work focuses on a novel approach that recognizes object motion using pixel-level visual information extracted from a single RGB image. Such visual cues capture changes in the object's position and scale, allowing the policy to reason about the object's motion. Furthermore, to achieve stable learning in a high-DoF system composed of a robot arm equipped with a multi-fingered hand, we design a heterogeneous multi-agent reinforcement learning framework that defines the arm and hand as independent agents with distinct roles. Each agent is trained cooperatively using role-specific observations and rewards, and the learned policies are successfully transferred from simulation to the real world.
tldr: The paper presents a multi-agent reinforcement learning approach for a robot to catch thrown objects using only pixel-level visual input from a single RGB camera and successfully transfers the learned policy from simulation to the real world.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种多智能体强化学习方法，使机器人仅使用来自单个RGB摄像头的像素级视觉输入来接住抛出的物体，并将学习到的策略成功地从仿真转移到现实世界。
summary_zh: 为了抓住抛掷物体，机器人需要能够及时感知物体的运动并生成控制动作。本研究没有显式估计物体的3D位置，而是专注于一种新颖的方法，该方法利用从单张RGB图像中提取的像素级视觉信息来识别物体的运动。这些视觉线索捕捉物体位置和尺度的变化，从而使策略能够推断物体的运动。此外，为了在高自由度系统中实现稳定的学习，该系统由装有多指手的机械臂组成，我们设计了一种异构的多智能体强化学习框架，将机械臂和手定义为具有不同角色的独立智能体。每个智能体使用角色特定的观察和奖励进行协同训练，学习到的策略已成功地从仿真环境迁移到真实世界。

title: Metamorphic Testing of Vision-Language Action-Enabled Robots
summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models are multimodal robotic task controllers that, given an instruction and visual inputs, produce a sequence of low-level control actions (or motor commands) enabling a robot to execute the requested task in the physical environment. These systems face the test oracle problem from multiple perspectives. On the one hand, a test oracle must be defined for each instruction prompt, which is a complex and non-generalizable approach. On the other hand, current state-of-the-art oracles typically capture symbolic representations of the world (e.g., robot and object states), enabling the correctness evaluation of a task, but fail to assess other critical aspects, such as the quality with which VLA-enabled robots perform a task. In this paper, we explore whether Metamorphic Testing (MT) can alleviate the test oracle problem in this context. To do so, we propose two metamorphic relation patterns and five metamorphic relations to assess whether changes to the test inputs impact the original trajectory of the VLA-enabled robots. An empirical study involving five VLA models, two simulated robots, and four robotic tasks shows that MT can effectively alleviate the test oracle problem by automatically detecting diverse types of failures, including, but not limited to, uncompleted tasks. More importantly, the proposed MRs are generalizable, making the proposed approach applicable across different VLA models, robots, and tasks, even in the absence of test oracles.
tldr: The paper proposes a Metamorphic Testing (MT) approach to address the test oracle problem in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models for robotics, demonstrating its effectiveness in detecting failures and generalizing across models, robots, and tasks.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种变形测试（MT）方法，旨在解决机器人中视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型的测试预言问题，证明了该方法在检测故障和推广到不同模型、机器人和任务中的有效性。
summary_zh: 视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型是一种多模态机器人任务控制器，它在给定指令和视觉输入的情况下，产生一系列低层控制动作（或电机指令），使机器人能够在物理环境中执行请求的任务。这些系统面临来自多个角度的测试判断问题。一方面，必须为每个指令提示定义一个测试判断标准，这是一种复杂且不具通用性的方法。另一方面，当前最先进的判断标准通常捕获世界的符号表示（例如，机器人和对象状态），从而能够评估任务的正确性，但未能评估其他关键方面，例如VLA赋能机器人执行任务的质量。在本文中，我们探讨了变质测试（MT）是否可以缓解此背景下的测试判断问题。为此，我们提出了两种变质关系模式和五种变质关系，以评估测试输入的更改是否会影响VLA赋能机器人的原始轨迹。一项涉及五个VLA模型、两个模拟机器人和四个机器人任务的实证研究表明，MT可以通过自动检测各种类型的故障（包括但不限于未完成的任务）来有效缓解测试判断问题。更重要的是，所提出的变质关系具有通用性，使得所提出的方法适用于不同的VLA模型、机器人和任务，即使在没有测试判断标准的情况下也是如此。

title: SignVLA: A Gloss-Free Vision-Language-Action Framework for Real-Time Sign Language-Guided Robotic Manipulation
summary:
We present, to our knowledge, the first sign language-driven Vision-Language-Action (VLA) framework for intuitive and inclusive human-robot interaction. Unlike conventional approaches that rely on gloss annotations as intermediate supervision, the proposed system adopts a gloss-free paradigm and directly maps visual sign gestures to semantic instructions. This design reduces annotation cost and avoids the information loss introduced by gloss representations, enabling more natural and scalable multimodal interaction.
  In this work, we focus on a real-time alphabet-level finger-spelling interface that provides a robust and low-latency communication channel for robotic control. Compared with large-scale continuous sign language recognition, alphabet-level interaction offers improved reliability, interpretability, and deployment feasibility in safety-critical embodied environments. The proposed pipeline transforms continuous gesture streams into coherent language commands through geometric normalization, temporal smoothing, and lexical refinement, ensuring stable and consistent interaction.
  Furthermore, the framework is designed to support future integration of transformer-based gloss-free sign language models, enabling scalable word-level and sentence-level semantic understanding. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed system in grounding sign-derived instructions into precise robotic actions under diverse interaction scenarios. These results highlight the potential of the framework to advance accessible, scalable, and multimodal embodied intelligence.
tldr: The paper introduces a novel vision-language-action framework that uses sign language (specifically finger-spelling) to control robots without relying on gloss annotations, enabling more natural and scalable human-robot interaction.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种新颖的视觉-语言-动作框架，该框架使用手语（特别是手指拼写）来控制机器人，无需依赖词汇注释，从而实现更自然和可扩展的人机交互。
summary_zh:
据我们所知，我们提出了首个手语驱动的视觉-语言-动作(VLA)框架，用于实现直观且包容的人机交互。与依赖于词汇注释作为中间监督的传统方法不同，所提出的系统采用无词汇范式，直接将视觉手势映射到语义指令。这种设计降低了标注成本，并避免了词汇表示引入的信息损失，从而实现了更自然和可扩展的多模态交互。

在这项工作中，我们专注于一个实时的字母级指语拼写界面，该界面为机器人控制提供了一个稳健且低延迟的通信通道。与大规模连续手语识别相比，字母级交互在安全攸关的具身环境中提供了更高的可靠性、可解释性和部署可行性。所提出的流程通过几何归一化、时间平滑和词汇提炼，将连续手势流转换为连贯的语言命令，从而确保稳定和一致的交互。

此外，该框架旨在支持未来集成基于Transformer的无词汇手语模型，从而实现可扩展的词级和句子级语义理解。实验结果表明，所提出的系统在不同交互场景下能够有效地将手语指令转化为精确的机器人动作。这些结果突显了该框架在推进可访问、可扩展和多模态具身智能方面的潜力。

title: When to Act, Ask, or Learn: Uncertainty-Aware Policy Steering
summary: Policy steering is an emerging way to adapt robot behaviors at deployment-time: a learned verifier analyzes low-level action samples proposed by a pre-trained policy (e.g., diffusion policy) and selects only those aligned with the task. While Vision-Language Models (VLMs) are promising general-purpose verifiers due to their reasoning capabilities, existing frameworks often assume these models are well-calibrated. In practice, the overconfident judgment from VLM can degrade the steering performance under both high-level semantic uncertainty in task specifications and low-level action uncertainty or incapability of the pre-trained policy. We propose uncertainty-aware policy steering (UPS), a framework that jointly reasons about semantic task uncertainty and low-level action feasibility, and selects an uncertainty resolution strategy: execute a high-confidence action, clarify task ambiguity via natural language queries, or ask for action interventions to correct the low-level policy when it is deemed incapable at the task. We leverage conformal prediction to calibrate the composition of the VLM and the pre-trained base policy, providing statistical assurances that the verifier selects the correct strategy. After collecting interventions during deployment, we employ residual learning to improve the capability of the pre-trained policy, enabling the system to learn continually but with minimal expensive human feedback. We demonstrate our framework through experiments in simulation and on hardware, showing that UPS can disentangle confident, ambiguous, and incapable scenarios and minimizes expensive user interventions compared to uncalibrated baselines and prior human- or robot-gated continual learning approaches. Videos can be found at https://jessie-yuan.github.io/ups/
tldr: This paper introduces Uncertainty-Aware Policy Steering (UPS), a framework using calibrated VLMs to adapt robot policies by deciding whether to execute actions, ask for clarification, or request interventions, and continually learns from these interventions.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了一种不确定性感知策略引导（UPS）框架，该框架使用校准的视觉-语言模型来调整机器人策略，从而决定是执行动作、请求澄清还是请求干预，并从这些干预中持续学习。
summary_zh: 策略引导是一种新兴的部署时调整机器人行为的方法：一个学习到的验证器分析预训练策略（例如，扩散策略）提出的底层动作样本，并仅选择与任务对齐的样本。尽管视觉-语言模型（VLMs）因其推理能力而成为有前景的通用验证器，但现有的框架通常假设这些模型具有良好的校准性。在实践中，VLMs过度自信的判断可能会降低策略引导的性能，这既体现在任务规范中的高层语义不确定性，也体现在预训练策略的底层动作不确定性或能力不足。我们提出了不确定性感知策略引导（UPS），该框架联合推理语义任务不确定性和底层动作可行性，并选择一种不确定性解决策略：执行高置信度的动作，通过自然语言查询澄清任务模糊性，或者在认为底层策略无法胜任该任务时请求动作干预以纠正该策略。我们利用共形预测来校准VLMs和预训练基础策略的组合，从而提供验证器选择正确策略的统计保证。在部署期间收集干预后，我们采用残差学习来提高预训练策略的能力，使系统能够持续学习，但只需要最少的人工昂贵反馈。我们通过仿真和硬件实验证明了我们的框架，表明UPS可以分离出自信、模糊和无能力的情况，并且与未校准的基线以及先前人工或机器人门控的持续学习方法相比，最大限度地减少了昂贵的用户干预。视频详见https://jessie-yuan.github.io/ups/

title: EgoAVFlow: Robot Policy Learning with Active Vision from Human Egocentric Videos via 3D Flow
summary: Egocentric human videos provide a scalable source of manipulation demonstrations; however, deploying them on robots requires active viewpoint control to maintain task-critical visibility, which human viewpoint imitation often fails to provide due to human-specific priors. We propose EgoAVFlow, which learns manipulation and active vision from egocentric videos through a shared 3D flow representation that supports geometric visibility reasoning and transfers without robot demonstrations. EgoAVFlow uses diffusion models to predict robot actions, future 3D flow, and camera trajectories, and refines viewpoints at test time with reward-maximizing denoising under a visibility-aware reward computed from predicted motion and scene geometry. Real-world experiments under actively changing viewpoints show that EgoAVFlow consistently outperforms prior human-demo-based baselines, demonstrating effective visibility maintenance and robust manipulation without robot demonstrations.
tldr: EgoAVFlow learns robot manipulation and active vision from human egocentric videos using a shared 3D flow representation, enabling effective visibility maintenance and robust manipulation without robot demonstrations, outperforming existing methods.
tldr_zh: EgoAVFlow 使用共享的 3D 流表示从人类以自我为中心的视频中学习机器人操作和主动视觉，从而无需机器人演示即可实现有效的可见性维护和鲁棒的操作，并且优于现有方法。
summary_zh: 第一人称人类视频提供了可扩展的操作演示来源；然而，将它们部署在机器人上需要主动视点控制以维持任务关键可见性，而人类视点模仿常常由于人类特有的先验知识而无法提供这种控制。我们提出了EgoAVFlow，它通过共享的3D光流表示从第一人称视频中学习操作和主动视觉，该光流表示支持几何可见性推理，且无需机器人演示即可转移。EgoAVFlow使用扩散模型来预测机器人动作、未来3D光流和相机轨迹，并在测试时通过奖励最大化降噪来优化视点，该降噪基于从预测运动和场景几何计算得到的可见性感知奖励。在主动变化视点下的真实世界实验表明，EgoAVFlow始终优于先前基于人类演示的基线方法，证明了其在无需机器人演示的情况下实现有效可见性维护和稳健操作的能力。

title: CWM: Contrastive World Models for Action Feasibility Learning in Embodied Agent Pipelines
summary: A reliable action feasibility scorer is a critical bottleneck in embodied agent pipelines: before any planning or reasoning occurs, the agent must identify which candidate actions are physically executable in the current state. Existing approaches use supervised fine-tuning (SFT) to train action scorers, but SFT treats each candidate independently and does not explicitly teach the model to discriminate between actions that are physically correct and those that are subtly wrong. We propose the Contrastive World Model (CWM), which fine-tunes a large language model (LLM) as an action scorer using an InfoNCE contrastive objective with hard-mined negative examples. The key idea is to push valid actions away from invalid ones in scoring space, with special emphasis on hard negatives: semantically similar but physically incompatible candidates. We evaluate CWM on the ScienceWorld benchmark through two studies. First, an intrinsic affordance evaluation on 605 hard-negative test pairs shows that CWM outperforms SFT by +6.76 percentage points on Precision@1 for minimal-edit negatives -- cases where a single word changes the physical outcome -- and achieves a higher AUC-ROC (0.929 vs. 0.906). Second, a live filter characterisation study measures how well CWM ranks gold-path actions against all valid environment actions during task execution. Under out-of-distribution stress conditions, CWM maintains a significantly better safety margin (-2.39) than SFT (-3.96), indicating that the gold action is ranked closer to the top. These results support the hypothesis that contrastive training induces representations that capture physical feasibility more faithfully than SFT alone.
tldr: The paper introduces Contrastive World Models (CWM), a method for fine-tuning LLMs to improve action feasibility scoring in embodied agents using a contrastive learning objective with hard negatives, demonstrating improved performance on the ScienceWorld benchmark compared to supervised fine-tuning.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了对比世界模型 (CWM)， 一种用于微调大型语言模型以提高具身智能体中动作可行性评分的方法， 使用带有难负样本的对比学习目标， 并在 ScienceWorld 基准测试中展示了比有监督微调更好的性能。
summary_zh: 一个可靠的动作可行性评分器是具身智能体流水线中的一个关键瓶颈：在进行任何规划或推理之前，智能体必须识别哪些候选动作在当前状态下是物理可执行的。现有的方法使用监督微调（SFT）来训练动作评分器，但SFT独立地处理每个候选动作，并没有明确地教导模型区分物理上正确的动作和那些细微错误的动作。我们提出了对比世界模型（CWM），它使用InfoNCE对比目标和挖掘的困难负样本来微调大型语言模型（LLM）作为动作评分器。关键思想是在评分空间中将有效动作从无效动作中推开，特别强调困难负例：语义相似但物理不兼容的候选动作。我们通过两个研究在ScienceWorld基准上评估CWM。首先，对605个困难负例测试对的内部可用性评估表明，对于最小编辑负例（即单个词改变物理结果的情况），CWM的Precision@1比SFT高+6.76个百分点，并且获得了更高的AUC-ROC（0.929 vs. 0.906）。其次，实时过滤特性研究测量了在任务执行期间，CWM将金牌路线动作与所有有效的环境动作进行排序的能力。在超出分布的压力条件下，CWM保持了明显更好的安全裕量（-2.39），而SFT为（-3.96），表明金牌动作的排名更接近顶部。这些结果支持了以下假设：对比学习训练诱导的表征比单独使用SFT更忠实地捕捉物理可行性。

title: Spatio-Temporal Token Pruning for Efficient High-Resolution GUI Agents
summary: Pure-vision GUI agents provide universal interaction capabilities but suffer from severe efficiency bottlenecks due to the massive spatiotemporal redundancy inherent in high-resolution screenshots and historical trajectories. We identify two critical misalignments in existing compression paradigms: the temporal mismatch, where uniform history encoding diverges from the agent's "fading memory" attention pattern, and the spatial topology conflict, where unstructured pruning compromises the grid integrity required for precise coordinate grounding, inducing spatial hallucinations. To address these challenges, we introduce GUIPruner, a training-free framework tailored for high-resolution GUI navigation. It synergizes Temporal-Adaptive Resolution (TAR), which eliminates historical redundancy via decay-based resizing, and Stratified Structure-aware Pruning (SSP), which prioritizes interactive foregrounds and semantic anchors while safeguarding global layout. Extensive evaluations across diverse benchmarks demonstrate that GUIPruner consistently achieves state-of-the-art performance, effectively preventing the collapse observed in large-scale models under high compression. Notably, on Qwen2-VL-2B, our method delivers a 3.4x reduction in FLOPs and a 3.3x speedup in vision encoding latency while retaining over 94% of the original performance, enabling real-time, high-precision navigation with minimal resource consumption.
tldr: The paper introduces GUIPruner, a training-free method for efficient high-resolution GUI navigation agents, which uses Temporal-Adaptive Resolution and Stratified Structure-aware Pruning to reduce FLOPs and latency while maintaining performance.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了GUIPruner，一种用于高效高分辨率GUI导航代理的免训练方法，该方法通过使用时间自适应分辨率和分层结构感知剪枝，在保持性能的同时减少FLOPs和延迟。
summary_zh: 纯视觉GUI智能体提供通用的交互能力，但由于高分辨率截图和历史轨迹中固有的巨大时空冗余，面临严重的效率瓶颈。我们指出了现有压缩范式中的两个关键错位：时间错配，即统一的历史编码与智能体的“衰减记忆”注意力模式相背离；以及空间拓扑冲突，即非结构化剪枝损害了精确坐标定位所需的网格完整性，从而导致空间幻觉。为了应对这些挑战，我们提出了GUIPruner，一个为高分辨率GUI导航量身定制的免训练框架。它协同了时间自适应分辨率（TAR），通过基于衰减的调整大小来消除历史冗余，以及分层结构感知剪枝（SSP），优先处理交互前景和语义锚点，同时保护全局布局。在多个基准测试上的广泛评估表明，GUIPruner始终如一地实现了最先进的性能，有效地防止了大型模型在高压缩下出现的性能崩溃。值得注意的是，在Qwen2-VL-2B上，我们的方法实现了3.4倍的FLOPs缩减和3.3倍的视觉编码延迟加速，同时保留了超过94%的原始性能，从而以最小的资源消耗实现了实时、高精度的导航。

title: Efficient Encoder-Free Fourier-based 3D Large Multimodal Model
summary: Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) that process 3D data typically rely on heavy, pre-trained visual encoders to extract geometric features. While recent 2D LMMs have begun to eliminate such encoders for efficiency and scalability, extending this paradigm to 3D remains challenging due to the unordered and large-scale nature of point clouds. This leaves a critical unanswered question: How can we design an LMM that tokenizes unordered 3D data effectively and efficiently without a cumbersome encoder? We propose Fase3D, the first efficient encoder-free Fourier-based 3D scene LMM. Fase3D tackles the challenges of scalability and permutation invariance with a novel tokenizer that combines point cloud serialization and the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) to approximate self-attention. This design enables an effective and computationally minimal architecture, built upon three key innovations: First, we represent large scenes compactly via structured superpoints. Second, our space-filling curve serialization followed by an FFT enables efficient global context modeling and graph-based token merging. Lastly, our Fourier-augmented LoRA adapters inject global frequency-aware interactions into the LLMs at a negligible cost. Fase3D achieves performance comparable to encoder-based 3D LMMs while being significantly more efficient in computation and parameters. Project website: https://tev-fbk.github.io/Fase3D.
tldr: This paper introduces Fase3D, an efficient encoder-free 3D Large Multimodal Model utilizing Fourier transforms for point cloud processing, achieving comparable performance to encoder-based methods with significantly reduced computational cost.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了Fase3D，一种高效的无编码器3D大型多模态模型，它利用傅里叶变换进行点云处理，在计算成本显著降低的情况下，性能与基于编码器的方法相当。
summary_zh: 处理3D数据的大型多模态模型(LMM)通常依赖于庞大的、预训练的视觉编码器来提取几何特征。虽然最近的2D LMM已经开始消除此类编码器以提高效率和可扩展性，但由于点云的无序性和大规模性，将这种范式扩展到3D仍然具有挑战性。这留下了一个关键的悬而未决的问题：我们如何在没有笨重编码器的情况下，设计一种能够有效且高效地标记化无序3D数据的LMM？我们提出了Fase3D，这是第一个基于傅里叶变换的、高效的无编码器3D场景LMM。 Fase3D通过一种新颖的标记器来解决可扩展性和置换不变性的挑战，该标记器结合了点云序列化和快速傅里叶变换(FFT)来近似自注意力。这种设计实现了一种有效且计算最小的架构，建立在三个关键创新之上：首先，我们通过结构化的超点来紧凑地表示大型场景。其次，我们的空间填充曲线序列化，然后进行FFT，从而实现高效的全局上下文建模和基于图的token合并。最后，我们的傅里叶增强型LoRA适配器以可忽略的代价将全局频率感知交互注入到LLM中。 Fase3D实现了与基于编码器的3D LMM相当的性能，同时在计算和参数方面效率更高。项目网站：https://tev-fbk.github.io/Fase3D。

title: The Trinity of Consistency as a Defining Principle for General World Models
summary: The construction of World Models capable of learning, simulating, and reasoning about objective physical laws constitutes a foundational challenge in the pursuit of Artificial General Intelligence. Recent advancements represented by video generation models like Sora have demonstrated the potential of data-driven scaling laws to approximate physical dynamics, while the emerging Unified Multimodal Model (UMM) offers a promising architectural paradigm for integrating perception, language, and reasoning. Despite these advances, the field still lacks a principled theoretical framework that defines the essential properties requisite for a General World Model. In this paper, we propose that a World Model must be grounded in the Trinity of Consistency: Modal Consistency as the semantic interface, Spatial Consistency as the geometric basis, and Temporal Consistency as the causal engine. Through this tripartite lens, we systematically review the evolution of multimodal learning, revealing a trajectory from loosely coupled specialized modules toward unified architectures that enable the synergistic emergence of internal world simulators. To complement this conceptual framework, we introduce CoW-Bench, a benchmark centered on multi-frame reasoning and generation scenarios. CoW-Bench evaluates both video generation models and UMMs under a unified evaluation protocol. Our work establishes a principled pathway toward general world models, clarifying both the limitations of current systems and the architectural requirements for future progress.
tldr: This paper proposes the 'Trinity of Consistency' (Modal, Spatial, and Temporal) as a guiding principle for General World Models and introduces CoW-Bench, a new benchmark for evaluating multi-frame reasoning and generation. It aims to provide a theoretical framework and benchmark for developing more robust and generalizable world models.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了“一致性三位一体”（模态一致性、空间一致性、时间一致性）作为通用世界模型的指导原则，并引入了CoW-Bench，这是一个用于评估多帧推理和生成的新基准。旨在为开发更鲁棒和可泛化的世界模型提供理论框架和基准。
summary_zh: 能够学习、模拟和推理客观物理定律的世界模型的构建，是通往通用人工智能的根本性挑战。以Sora为代表的视频生成模型所取得的最新进展，展示了数据驱动的缩放定律在近似物理动力学方面的潜力，而新兴的统一多模态模型(UMM)为整合感知、语言和推理提供了一种有希望的架构范式。尽管取得了这些进展，但该领域仍然缺乏一个原则性的理论框架来定义通用世界模型所必需的本质属性。在本文中，我们提出世界模型必须植根于一致性三位一体：模态一致性作为语义接口，空间一致性作为几何基础，以及时间一致性作为因果引擎。通过这个三方视角，我们系统地回顾了多模态学习的演变，揭示了一条从松散耦合的专业模块到统一架构的轨迹，这些统一架构能够协同涌现内部世界模拟器。为了补充这个概念框架，我们引入了CoW-Bench，一个以多帧推理和生成场景为中心的基准测试。CoW-Bench在一个统一的评估协议下评估视频生成模型和UMM。我们的工作建立了一条通往通用世界模型的原则性途径，明确了现有系统的局限性以及未来进展的架构需求。

title: On Sample-Efficient Generalized Planning via Learned Transition Models
summary: Generalized planning studies the construction of solution strategies that generalize across families of planning problems sharing a common domain model, formally defined by a transition function $γ: S \times A \rightarrow S$. Classical approaches achieve such generalization through symbolic abstractions and explicit reasoning over $γ$. In contrast, recent Transformer-based planners, such as PlanGPT and Plansformer, largely cast generalized planning as direct action-sequence prediction, bypassing explicit transition modeling. While effective on in-distribution instances, these approaches typically require large datasets and model sizes, and often suffer from state drift in long-horizon settings due to the absence of explicit world-state evolution. In this work, we formulate generalized planning as a transition-model learning problem, in which a neural model explicitly approximates the successor-state function $\hatγ \approx γ$ and generates plans by rolling out symbolic state trajectories. Instead of predicting actions directly, the model autoregressively predicts intermediate world states, thereby learning the domain dynamics as an implicit world model. To study size-invariant generalization and sample efficiency, we systematically evaluate multiple state representations and neural architectures, including relational graph encodings. Our results show that learning explicit transition models yields higher out-of-distribution satisficing-plan success than direct action-sequence prediction in multiple domains, while achieving these gains with significantly fewer training instances and smaller models. This is an extended version of a short paper accepted at ICAPS 2026 under the same title.
tldr: The paper proposes learning explicit transition models for generalized planning, showing improved sample efficiency and out-of-distribution generalization compared to direct action-sequence prediction methods.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出通过学习显式转换模型来进行泛化规划，结果表明与直接动作序列预测方法相比，该方法具有更高的样本效率和更好的超出分布泛化能力。
summary_zh: 广义规划研究的是构建跨越共享共同领域模型的规划问题族的通用求解策略，这些问题族在形式上由转移函数$γ: S \times A \rightarrow S$定义。 经典方法通过符号抽象和对$γ$的显式推理来实现这种泛化。 相比之下，最近基于Transformer的规划器，如PlanGPT和Plansformer，很大程度上将广义规划视为直接行动序列预测，绕过了显式转移建模。 尽管这些方法在同分布实例上有效，但通常需要大型数据集和模型规模，并且由于缺乏显式的世界状态演化，经常在长程环境中遭受状态漂移。 在这项工作中，我们将广义规划形式化为转移模型学习问题，其中神经模型显式地近似后继状态函数$\hatγ \approx γ$，并通过展开符号状态轨迹来生成规划。 该模型不是直接预测行动，而是自回归地预测中间世界状态，从而学习领域动态作为隐式世界模型。 为了研究规模不变的泛化和样本效率，我们系统地评估了多种状态表示和神经架构，包括关系图编码。 我们的结果表明，在多个领域中，学习显式转移模型比直接行动序列预测产生更高的异分布可满足规划成功率，同时以显著更少的训练实例和更小的模型实现这些收益。 这是在ICAPS 2026 上接受的同名短论文的扩展版本。

title: Exploratory Memory-Augmented LLM Agent via Hybrid On- and Off-Policy Optimization
summary: Exploration remains the key bottleneck for large language model agents trained with reinforcement learning. While prior methods exploit pretrained knowledge, they fail in environments requiring the discovery of novel states. We propose Exploratory Memory-Augmented On- and Off-Policy Optimization (EMPO$^2$), a hybrid RL framework that leverages memory for exploration and combines on- and off-policy updates to make LLMs perform well with memory while also ensuring robustness without it. On ScienceWorld and WebShop, EMPO$^2$ achieves 128.6% and 11.3% improvements over GRPO, respectively. Moreover, in out-of-distribution tests, EMPO$^2$ demonstrates superior adaptability to new tasks, requiring only a few trials with memory and no parameter updates. These results highlight EMPO$^2$ as a promising framework for building more exploratory and generalizable LLM-based agents.
tldr: The paper introduces EMPO$^2$, a novel reinforcement learning framework that uses memory and a hybrid on/off-policy approach to improve exploration and generalization in LLM agents, achieving significant performance gains in ScienceWorld and WebShop environments.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了EMPO$^2$，一种新颖的强化学习框架，它使用记忆和混合的on/off-policy方法来提高LLM agent的探索和泛化能力，并在ScienceWorld和WebShop环境中取得了显著的性能提升。
summary_zh: 探索仍然是使用强化学习训练的大型语言模型代理的主要瓶颈。虽然先前的方法利用了预训练知识，但它们在需要发现新状态的环境中表现不佳。我们提出了探索性记忆增强型在线和离线策略优化（EMPO$^2$），这是一个混合RL框架，利用记忆进行探索，并结合在线和离线策略更新，使LLM在使用记忆的同时表现良好，并确保在没有记忆的情况下也具有鲁棒性。在ScienceWorld和WebShop上，EMPO$^2$分别实现了比GRPO高128.6%和11.3%的提升。此外，在分布外测试中，EMPO$^2$展示了对新任务的卓越适应性，仅需少量使用记忆的试验且无需参数更新。这些结果表明，EMPO$^2$ 是构建更具探索性和泛化能力的基于LLM的代理的有前景的框架。

title: FactGuard: Agentic Video Misinformation Detection via Reinforcement Learning
summary: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have substantially advanced video misinformation detection through unified multimodal reasoning, but they often rely on fixed-depth inference and place excessive trust in internally generated assumptions, particularly in scenarios where critical evidence is sparse, fragmented, or requires external verification. To address these limitations, we propose FactGuard, an agentic framework for video misinformation detection that formulates verification as an iterative reasoning process built upon MLLMs. FactGuard explicitly assesses task ambiguity and selectively invokes external tools to acquire critical evidence, enabling progressive refinement of reasoning trajectories. To further strengthen this capability, we introduce a two-stage training strategy that combines domain-specific agentic supervised fine-tuning with decision-aware reinforcement learning to optimize tool usage and calibrate risk-sensitive decision making. Extensive experiments on FakeSV, FakeTT, and FakeVV demonstrate FactGuard's state-of-the-art performance and validate its excellent robustness and generalization capacity.
tldr: FactGuard is a reinforcement learning-based agentic framework for video misinformation detection that utilizes external tools to refine reasoning and improve robustness, achieving state-of-the-art performance on multiple datasets.
tldr_zh: FactGuard是一个基于强化学习的Agent框架，用于视频错误信息检测。它利用外部工具来改进推理和提高鲁棒性，并在多个数据集上实现了最先进的性能。
summary_zh: 多模态大型语言模型（MLLM）通过统一的多模态推理显著推进了视频虚假信息检测，但它们通常依赖于固定深度的推理，并且过度信任内部生成的假设，尤其是在关键证据稀疏、碎片化或需要外部验证的情况下。为解决这些局限性，我们提出了 FactGuard，一个用于视频虚假信息检测的代理框架，它将验证构建为基于 MLLM 的迭代推理过程。FactGuard 显式评估任务模糊性，并选择性地调用外部工具来获取关键证据，从而实现推理轨迹的渐进式优化。为了进一步增强这种能力，我们引入了一种两阶段训练策略，该策略结合了特定领域的代理式监督微调与决策感知强化学习，以优化工具使用并校准风险敏感型决策。在 FakeSV、FakeTT 和 FakeVV 上的大量实验表明，FactGuard 具有最先进的性能，并验证了其卓越的鲁棒性和泛化能力。

title: Hierarchy-of-Groups Policy Optimization for Long-Horizon Agentic Tasks
summary: Group-based reinforcement learning (RL), such as GRPO, has advanced the capabilities of large language models on long-horizon agentic tasks. To enable more fine-grained policy updates, recent research has increasingly shifted toward stepwise group-based policy optimization, which treats each step in a rollout trajectory independently while using a memory module to retain historical context. However, we find a key issue in estimating stepwise relative advantages, namely context inconsistency, where steps within the same group may differ in their historical contexts. Empirically, we reveal that this issue can lead to severely biased advantage estimation, thereby degrading policy optimization significantly. To address the issue, in this paper, we propose Hierarchy-of-Groups Policy Optimization (HGPO) for long-horizon agentic tasks. Specifically, within a group of rollout trajectories, HGPO assigns each step to multiple hierarchical groups according to the consistency of historical contexts. Then, for each step, HGPO computes distinct advantages within each group and aggregates them with an adaptive weighting scheme. In this way, HGPO can achieve a favorable bias-variance trade-off in stepwise advantage estimation, without extra models or rollouts. Evaluations on two challenging agentic tasks, ALFWorld and WebShop with Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct and Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, show that HGPO significantly outperforms existing agentic RL methods under the same computational constraints. Code is available at https://github.com/langfengQ/verl-agent/tree/master/recipe/hgpo.
tldr: The paper introduces Hierarchy-of-Groups Policy Optimization (HGPO) to address context inconsistency in stepwise group-based RL for long-horizon agentic tasks, showing significant performance improvements on ALFWorld and WebShop using Qwen2.5.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了层级分组策略优化（HGPO），旨在解决长程代理任务中逐步分组强化学习的背景不一致问题，并在使用Qwen2.5的ALFWorld和WebShop上显示出显著的性能提升。
summary_zh: 基于群组的强化学习（RL），例如GRPO，已经提升了大型语言模型在长程自主agent任务上的能力。为了实现更精细的策略更新，近期的研究越来越多地转向逐步式基于群组的策略优化，即独立处理rollout轨迹中的每一步，同时利用记忆模块来保留历史上下文。然而，我们发现估计逐步式相对优势时存在一个关键问题，即上下文不一致性，同一群组内的步骤可能在历史上下文中存在差异。经验表明，这个问题会导致严重的优势估计偏差，从而显著降低策略优化的效果。为了解决这个问题，在本文中，我们提出了用于长程自主agent任务的分层群组策略优化（HGPO）。具体而言，在rollout轨迹群组中，HGPO根据历史上下文的一致性将每一步分配到多个分层群组中。然后，对于每一步，HGPO在每个群组内计算不同的优势，并通过自适应加权方案将它们聚合。通过这种方式，HGPO可以在逐步式优势估计中实现有利的偏差-方差权衡，而无需额外的模型或rollout。在两个具有挑战性的自主agent任务ALFWorld和WebShop上，使用Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct和Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct进行的评估表明，HGPO在相同计算约束下显著优于现有的自主agent RL方法。代码可在以下网址获取：https://github.com/langfengQ/verl-agent/tree/master/recipe/hgpo.

title: MiroFlow: Towards High-Performance and Robust Open-Source Agent Framework for General Deep Research Tasks
summary: Despite the remarkable progress of large language models (LLMs), the capabilities of standalone LLMs have begun to plateau when tackling real-world, complex tasks that require interaction with external tools and dynamic environments. Although recent agent frameworks aim to enhance model autonomy through tool integration and external interaction, they still suffer from naive workflows, unstable performance, limited support across diverse benchmarks and tasks, and heavy reliance on costly commercial APIs. In this work, we propose a high-performance and robust open-source agent framework, termed MiroFlow, which incorporates an agent graph for flexible orchestration, an optional deep reasoning mode to enhance performance, and a robust workflow execution to ensure stable and reproducible performance. Extensive experiments demonstrate that MiroFlow consistently achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple agent benchmarks, including GAIA, BrowseComp-EN/ZH, HLE, xBench-DeepSearch, and notably FutureX. We hope it could serve as an easily accessible, reproducible, and comparable baseline for the deep research community.
tldr: MiroFlow is a new open-source agent framework that aims to improve performance, robustness, and accessibility for deep research tasks by using an agent graph, deep reasoning mode, and robust workflow execution, achieving state-of-the-art results on several benchmarks.
tldr_zh: MiroFlow是一个新的开源代理框架，旨在通过使用代理图、深度推理模式和稳健的工作流程执行来提高深度研究任务的性能、稳健性和可访问性，并在多个基准测试中实现了最先进的结果。
summary_zh: 尽管大型语言模型（LLM）取得了显著进展，但在处理需要与外部工具和动态环境交互的真实世界复杂任务时，独立LLM的能力已开始触及瓶颈。  尽管最近的代理框架旨在通过工具集成和外部交互来增强模型的自主性，但它们仍然存在工作流程简单、性能不稳定、对各种基准和任务的支持有限，以及严重依赖昂贵的商业API等问题。  在这项工作中，我们提出了一个高性能且稳健的开源代理框架，名为MiroFlow，它包含一个用于灵活编排的代理图、一个用于增强性能的可选深度推理模式以及一个用于确保稳定和可复现性能的稳健型工作流程执行。  大量实验表明，MiroFlow在多个代理基准测试中始终达到最先进的性能，包括GAIA、BrowseComp-EN/ZH、HLE、xBench-DeepSearch，尤其是在FutureX上。  我们希望它可以作为深度研究社区易于访问、可复现和可比较的基准。

title: AMA-Bench: Evaluating Long-Horizon Memory for Agentic Applications
summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are deployed as autonomous agents in increasingly complex applications, where enabling long-horizon memory is critical for achieving strong performance. However, a significant gap exists between practical applications and current evaluation standards for agent memory: existing benchmarks primarily focus on dialogue-centric, human-agent interactions. In reality, agent memory consists of a continuous stream of agent-environment interactions that are primarily composed of machine-generated representations. To bridge this gap, we introduce AMA-Bench (Agent Memory with Any length), which evaluates long-horizon memory for LLMs in real agentic applications. It features two key components: (1) a set of real-world agentic trajectories across representative agentic applications, paired with expert-curated QA, and (2) a set of synthetic agentic trajectories that scale to arbitrary horizons, paired with rule-based QA. Our comprehensive study shows that existing memory systems underperform on AMA-Bench primarily because they lack causality and objective information and are constrained by the lossy nature of similarity-based retrieval employed by many memory systems. To address these limitations, we propose AMA-Agent, an effective memory system featuring a causality graph and tool-augmented retrieval. Our results demonstrate that AMA-Agent achieves 57.22% average accuracy on AMA-Bench, surpassing the strongest memory system baselines by 11.16%.
tldr: The paper introduces AMA-Bench, a new benchmark for evaluating long-horizon memory in LLM-based agents, highlighting the shortcomings of existing memory systems and proposing AMA-Agent, a new memory system that leverages causality graphs and tool-augmented retrieval.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了AMA-Bench，一个新的用于评估基于LLM的Agent中长时记忆的基准，强调了现有记忆系统的缺点，并提出了一种新的记忆系统AMA-Agent，它利用因果图和工具增强检索。
summary_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）正被部署为自主智能体，应用于日益复杂的应用中，其中使智能体具备长程记忆能力对于实现卓越性能至关重要。然而，在实际应用与当前智能体记忆评估标准之间存在显著差距：现有基准主要关注以对话为中心的人机交互。实际上，智能体的记忆由持续的智能体-环境交互流组成，这些交互主要由机器生成的表征构成。为了弥合这一差距，我们引入了AMA-Bench（任意长度智能体记忆基准），该基准旨在评估大型语言模型在真实智能体应用中的长程记忆能力。它包含两个关键组成部分：（1）一套涵盖代表性智能体应用的真实世界智能体轨迹，并配有专家策划的问答；（2）一套可扩展至任意时间跨度的合成智能体轨迹，并配备基于规则的问答。我们全面的研究表明，现有记忆系统在AMA-Bench上的表现不佳，主要原因是它们缺乏因果关系和客观信息，并且受到许多记忆系统采用的基于相似性的检索的损失性质的限制。为了解决这些局限性，我们提出了AMA-Agent，这是一种有效的记忆系统，具有因果关系图和工具增强的检索能力。我们的结果表明，AMA-Agent在AMA-Bench上实现了57.22%的平均准确率，比最强的记忆系统基线高出11.16%。

title: Know What You Know: Metacognitive Entropy Calibration for Verifiable RL Reasoning
summary: Large reasoning models (LRMs) have emerged as a powerful paradigm for solving complex real-world tasks. In practice, these models are predominantly trained via Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR), yet most existing outcome-only RLVR pipelines rely almost exclusively on a binary correctness signal and largely ignore the model's intrinsic uncertainty. We term this discrepancy the uncertainty-reward mismatch, under which high- and low-uncertainty solutions are treated equivalently, preventing the policy from "Know What You Know" and impeding the shift from optimizing for correct answers to optimizing effective reasoning paths. This limitation is especially critical in reasoning-centric tasks such as mathematics and question answering, where performance hinges on the quality of the model's internal reasoning process rather than mere memorization of final answers. To address this, we propose EGPO, a metacognitive entropy calibration framework that explicitly integrates intrinsic uncertainty into RLVR for enhancing LRMs. EGPO estimates per-sample uncertainty using a zero-overhead entropy proxy derived from token-level likelihoods and aligns it with extrinsic correctness through an asymmetric calibration mechanism that preserves correct reasoning while selectively regulating overconfident failures, thereby enabling stable and uncertainty-aware policy optimization. Moreover, EGPO recovers informative learning signals from otherwise degenerate group-based rollouts without modifying the verifier or reward definition. Extensive experiments across multiple benchmarks demonstrate that the proposed EGPO leads to substantial and consistent improvements in reasoning performance, establishing a principled path for advancing LRMs through metacognitive entropy calibration.
tldr: This paper introduces EGPO, a metacognitive entropy calibration framework for training Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) with Reinforcement Learning and Verifiable Rewards (RLVR). It addresses the uncertainty-reward mismatch by explicitly incorporating intrinsic uncertainty to improve reasoning performance.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了一种名为EGPO的元认知熵校准框架，用于使用强化学习和可验证奖励（RLVR）训练大型推理模型(LRM)。它通过显式地结合内在不确定性来改进推理性能，从而解决不确定性-奖励不匹配的问题。
summary_zh: 大型推理模型（LRM）已成为解决复杂现实世界任务的强大范式。实践中，这些模型主要通过具有可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）进行训练，然而，目前大多数仅基于结果的RLVR流程几乎完全依赖于二元正确性信号，并且很大程度上忽略了模型固有的不确定性。我们将这种差异称为不确定性-奖励失配，在这种失配下，高不确定性和低不确定性的解决方案被同等对待，从而阻止策略“了解自己所知”，并阻碍了从优化正确答案到优化有效推理路径的转变。这种局限性在以推理为中心的任务中尤为关键，例如数学和问答，其中性能取决于模型内部推理过程的质量，而不仅仅是最终答案的记忆。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了 EGPO，一种元认知熵校准框架，它明确地将内在不确定性集成到 RLVR 中，以增强 LRM。EGPO 使用从 token 级似然性导出的零开销熵代理来估计每个样本的不确定性，并通过不对称校准机制将其与外在正确性对齐，该机制保留正确的推理，同时有选择地调节过度自信的失败，从而实现稳定且具有不确定性意识的策略优化。此外，EGPO 从原本退化的基于组的 rollout 中恢复信息丰富的学习信号，而无需修改验证器或奖励定义。在多个基准测试中进行的大量实验表明，所提出的 EGPO 在推理性能方面带来了显著且一致的改进，为通过元认知熵校准推进 LRM 建立了一条原则性途径。

title: AgentSentry: Mitigating Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents via Temporal Causal Diagnostics and Context Purification
summary: Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly rely on external tools and retrieval systems to autonomously complete complex tasks. However, this design exposes agents to indirect prompt injection (IPI), where attacker-controlled context embedded in tool outputs or retrieved content silently steers agent actions away from user intent. Unlike prompt-based attacks, IPI unfolds over multi-turn trajectories, making malicious control difficult to disentangle from legitimate task execution. Existing inference-time defenses primarily rely on heuristic detection and conservative blocking of high-risk actions, which can prematurely terminate workflows or broadly suppress tool usage under ambiguous multi-turn scenarios. We propose AgentSentry, a novel inference-time detection and mitigation framework for tool-augmented LLM agents. To the best of our knowledge, AgentSentry is the first inference-time defense to model multi-turn IPI as a temporal causal takeover. It localizes takeover points via controlled counterfactual re-executions at tool-return boundaries and enables safe continuation through causally guided context purification that removes attack-induced deviations while preserving task-relevant evidence. We evaluate AgentSentry on the \textsc{AgentDojo} benchmark across four task suites, three IPI attack families, and multiple black-box LLMs. AgentSentry eliminates successful attacks and maintains strong utility under attack, achieving an average Utility Under Attack (UA) of 74.55 %, improving UA by 20.8 to 33.6 percentage points over the strongest baselines without degrading benign performance.
tldr: The paper introduces AgentSentry, a defense mechanism against indirect prompt injection attacks in LLM agents using temporal causal diagnostics and context purification, showing significant utility improvements over existing baselines.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了AgentSentry，一种利用时间因果诊断和上下文净化来防御LLM智能体中存在的间接提示注入攻击的防御机制，与现有基线相比，该机制显示出显著的效用提升。
summary_zh: 大型语言模型(LLM)代理越来越多地依赖外部工具和检索系统来自主完成复杂任务。然而，这种设计使代理暴露于间接提示注入(IPI)的风险中，即攻击者控制的上下文嵌入在工具输出或检索到的内容中，悄无声息地引导代理行为偏离用户意图。与基于提示的攻击不同，IPI在多轮轨迹中展开，使得恶意控制难以与合法的任务执行区分开来。现有的推理时防御主要依赖于启发式检测和对高风险行为的保守性阻止，这可能会过早地终止工作流程或在模棱两可的多轮场景下广泛抑制工具的使用。我们提出了AgentSentry，一种用于工具增强型LLM代理的全新推理时检测和缓解框架。据我们所知，AgentSentry是第一个将多轮IPI建模为时间因果接管的推理时防御。它通过在工具返回边界进行受控的反事实重新执行来定位接管点，并通过因果引导的上下文净化实现安全延续，该净化移除攻击诱导的偏差，同时保留与任务相关的证据。我们在\textsc{AgentDojo}基准测试中，针对四个任务套件、三个IPI攻击家族和多个黑盒LLM评估了AgentSentry。AgentSentry消除了成功的攻击，并在攻击下保持强大的效用，实现了平均74.55%的攻击下效用(UA)，在不降低良性性能的情况下，比最强的基线提高了20.8到33.6个百分点。

title: SoPE: Spherical Coordinate-Based Positional Embedding for Enhancing Spatial Perception of 3D LVLMs
summary: 3D Large Vision-Language Models (3D LVLMs) built upon Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved remarkable progress across various multimodal tasks. However, their inherited position-dependent modeling mechanism, Rotary Position Embedding (RoPE), remains suboptimal for 3D multimodal understanding. The vanilla RoPE formulation fails to preserve essential three-dimensional spatial structures when encoding 3D tokens, and its relative distance computation overlooks angular dependencies, hindering the model's ability to capture directional variations in visual representations. To overcome these limitations, we introduce Spherical Coordinate-based Positional Embedding (SoPE). Our method maps point-cloud token indices into a 3D spherical coordinate space, enabling unified modeling of spatial locations and directional angles. This formulation preserves the inherent geometric structure of point-cloud data, enhances spatial awareness, and yields more consistent and expressive geometric representations for multimodal learning. In addition, we introduce a multi-scale frequency mixing strategy to fuse feature information across different frequency domains. Experimental results on multiple 3D scene benchmarks validate the effectiveness of our approach, while real-world deployment experiments further demonstrate its strong generalization capability.
tldr: The paper introduces Spherical Coordinate-based Positional Embedding (SoPE), a novel method that maps point-cloud token indices into a 3D spherical coordinate space to improve spatial awareness and geometric representation in 3D Large Vision-Language Models.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种基于球坐标的位置嵌入 (SoPE) 方法，该方法将点云 token 索引映射到 3D 球坐标空间，从而提高 3D 大视觉语言模型中的空间感知和几何表示。
summary_zh: 基于大型语言模型（LLMs）构建的3D大型视觉-语言模型（3D LVLMs）在各种多模态任务中取得了显著进展。然而，其继承的位置相关建模机制，即旋转位置嵌入（RoPE），对于3D多模态理解而言仍然欠佳。原始的RoPE公式在编码3D令牌时无法保留重要的三维空间结构，并且其相对距离计算忽略了角度依赖性，从而阻碍了模型捕获视觉表示中方向变化的能力。为了克服这些局限性，我们引入了基于球坐标的位置嵌入（SoPE）。我们的方法将点云令牌索引映射到3D球坐标空间，从而实现空间位置和方向角的统一建模。这种公式保留了点云数据固有的几何结构，增强了空间感知能力，并为多模态学习产生了更一致和更具表现力的几何表示。此外，我们引入了一种多尺度频率混合策略，以融合不同频率域的特征信息。在多个3D场景基准测试上的实验结果验证了我们方法的有效性，而真实世界的部署实验进一步证明了其强大的泛化能力。

title: Reinforcing Real-world Service Agents: Balancing Utility and Cost in Task-oriented Dialogue
summary: The rapid evolution of Large Language Models (LLMs) has accelerated the transition from conversational chatbots to general agents. However, effectively balancing empathetic communication with budget-aware decision-making remains an open challenge. Since existing methods fail to capture these complex strategic trade-offs, we propose InteractCS-RL, a framework that reframes task-oriented dialogue as a multi-granularity reinforcement learning process. Specifically, we first establish a User-centric Interaction Framework to provide a high-fidelity training gym, enabling agents to dynamically explore diverse strategies with persona-driven users. Then, we introduce Cost-aware Multi-turn Policy Optimization (CMPO) with a hybrid advantage estimation strategy. By integrating generative process credits and employing a PID-Lagrangian cost controller, CMPO effectively guides the policy to explore Pareto boundary between user reward and global cost constraints. Extensive experiments on customized real business scenarios demonstrate that InteractCS-RL significantly outperform other baselines across three evaluation dimensions. Further evaluation on tool-agent-user interaction benchmarks verify InteractCS-RL robustness across diverse domains.
tldr: This paper introduces InteractCS-RL, a reinforcement learning framework for task-oriented dialogue agents that balances user reward and cost constraints by using a user-centric interaction framework and cost-aware policy optimization. It shows improved performance in real business scenarios and tool-agent-user interaction benchmarks.
tldr_zh: 本文介绍了InteractCS-RL，一个用于面向任务的对话代理的强化学习框架，通过使用以用户为中心的交互框架和成本感知的策略优化，来平衡用户奖励和成本约束。 该框架在实际业务场景和工具-代理-用户交互基准测试中表现出改进的性能。
summary_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）的快速发展加速了从对话式聊天机器人向通用智能体的转变。然而，如何在共情交流与具有预算意识的决策之间取得有效平衡仍然是一个开放的挑战。由于现有方法未能捕捉到这些复杂的战略权衡，我们提出了InteractCS-RL，一个将面向任务的对话重新定义为多粒度强化学习过程的框架。具体而言，我们首先建立了一个以用户为中心的交互框架，以提供高保真的训练环境，使智能体能够与角色驱动的用户动态地探索各种策略。然后，我们引入了具有混合优势估计策略的成本感知多轮策略优化（CMPO）。通过整合生成过程信用并采用PID-拉格朗日成本控制器，CMPO有效地引导策略探索用户奖励与全局成本约束之间的帕累托边界。在定制的真实商业场景中进行的大量实验表明，InteractCS-RL在三个评估维度上显著优于其他基线。在工具-智能体-用户交互基准上的进一步评估验证了InteractCS-RL在不同领域的鲁棒性。

title: SUPERGLASSES: Benchmarking Vision Language Models as Intelligent Agents for AI Smart Glasses
summary: The rapid advancement of AI-powered smart glasses, one of the hottest wearable devices, has unlocked new frontiers for multimodal interaction, with Visual Question Answering (VQA) over external knowledge sources emerging as a core application.
